title: After-Action Report — Finisher stats (TO/QF/LR) + almanac wiring
scope: Two pieces: 1. Finisher counting stats — Terminal Outs (TO), Quality Finish (QF), and Lead-Retention % (LR%) — built on the relief entry-context spine. 2. Almanac wiring — every new stat from this arc (RISP, bunting, and the full relief/finisher suite) now flows through the almanac's own aggregation pipeline onto its player pages and leaderboards, matching the live app.

rationale.item: They measure who seals games in O27's continuous 27-out half, scaling with the "back-stage finisher-starter" strategy rather than assuming a one-inning closer.

O27 is one 27-out half per side, so the team that fields second is the one protecting a lead — all "finishing" is a second-half-fielding phenomenon, and a back-stage starter who enters with a lead and goes deep racks up TO exactly like a great closer would. The opening starter enters 0-0 (no lead) and so earns no TO, which is correct: TO measures closing, not opening.
Latent bug: the game-end pitching spell flush never recorded ir_inherited/ir_scored, so closers' inherited runners were being dropped.
Career follow-on: the almanac's career leaderboards aggregate the durable per-season snapshot tables, which didn't carry the new stats — so career (multi-season) views couldn't show them.

actions.description: Engine spine: SpellRecord gains entry_lead, min_lead, finished; pa.apply_event lazily captures entry lead and tracks running minimum; game._close_spell flushes half-finisher with finished=1; manager.pitching_change flushes replaced arms with finished=0
sim.py derives TO, QF, lead_entries/lead_held per spell, sums to the game row; persisted on game_pitcher_stats (+ migration, dedup SQL); LR% computed in _aggregate_pitcher_rows
Surfaced on the player-card Relief & Finisher panel, the Stat Browser pitching views, and the glossary
Fixed latent IR bug: game-end pitching spell flush now records ir_inherited/ir_scored

Almanac wiring: compute._BATTING_SUM_FIELDS += RISP and bunting fields; _PITCHING_SUM_FIELDS += relief fields; _augment_batters computes RISP slash + risp_conv + bunt_avg; _augment_pitchers computes ir_stop_pct, lr_pct, late_er_per_bf
Almanac player page: new Clutch · RISP, Small Ball · Bunting, and Relief & Finisher sections; leaderboards: RISP and Small Ball on the Situational board, Relief & Finisher on the Pitching board
Career archive wiring: schema + migration adding RISP/bunting columns to season_player_batting and relief/finisher columns to season_player_pitching (existing archives backfill to 0)
Rollover snapshot SUMs the new columns from the live game tables into the snapshot rows

Almanac career compute folds component sums across seasons and recomputes career rates (RISP slash + risp_conv, lra 0–10), ranks new boards; career template adds SH / Bunt H / Squeeze RBI / RISP RBI / RISP OPS (batting) and Terminal Outs / Quality Finish / LRA (pitching)

risk_posture: `gmLI` is intentionally **not** in the almanac (it needs the WPA model the almanac doesn't run); it lives on the live leaders + player card.
Team-level RISP/bunting/walkback/usage-shape were left to the live `/teams/stats` page rather than duplicated on the almanac team page.
A career IR-Stop% board was skipped — the rank macro formats `r[key]` directly and can't append `%`; the counting boards + LRA are the meaningful career reads.

validation_claims.claim: Fresh 150-game sim: finisher capture invariant-clean: lead_held ≤ lead_entries (0 violations); TO ≈ 4.8/game, QF ≈ 0.16/game; top-TO arms read as finisher-starters (e.g. 54 TO over 81 outs).
validation_claims.check_method: Run 150-game sim and audit finisher columns
Compare league IR totals pre/post fix on a 150-game sim

validation_claims.claim: All 1312 almanac player pages render; the RISP / Small Ball / Relief & Finisher blocks and the new leaderboard sections all render 200.
o27/tests + o27/almanac/tests + records/streaks (158) pass; all 11 stat invariants pass on the simmed DB.
validation_claims.check_method: Run pytest suites and the stat invariant suite

validation_claims.claim: Career follow-on: verified by simming a season, running the snapshot, and rendering the career page: columns persist with real values and all eight new boards render. 172 tests + 11 invariants pass.
validation_claims.check_method: Sim season, run snapshot, render career page, run tests

non_actions.reason: it needs the WPA model the almanac doesn't run

non_actions.item: Team-level RISP/bunting/walkback/usage-shape on the almanac team page
non_actions.reason: left to the live /teams/stats page rather than duplicated
the rank macro formats r[key] directly and can't append %; the counting boards + LRA are the meaningful career reads

non_actions.item: Extending player_career_lines (Hall of Fame and Streaks & Records cross-season records via records.py) with the new columns
non_actions.reason: a separate extension if those records should also carry RISP / bunting / relief

residual_risks: The IR "scored" attribution remains the documented heuristic from the prior pass (can mis-credit by one on a rare mixed score/out event).
min_lead-based "never relinquished" treats a tie (lead = 0) as relinquished for TO (strict lead required) but acceptable for QF (lead-or-tie).
LR% credits holding the lead on his watch, not strictly "to the 27th out".
Needs a resim to populate (sim-time capture), as with any box-stat addition.
open_hooks: player_career_lines extension for RISP/bunting/relief in HoF and cross-season records
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Finisher stats (TO/QF/LR) + almanac wiring
A note on O27's structure (why this is the right model)
unmapped_content:
**Branch:** `claude/friendly-hypatia-ShSHm`

Stat definitions: Terminal Outs = outs in a spell entered with a lead (entry_lead > 0), never relinquished (min_lead > 0), and finished. Quality Finish = finished with ≥ 9 outs while never trailing (min_lead ≥ 0). LR% = lead_held / lead_entries. The almanac reads game_*_stats directly (cached by DB mtime), so the new columns appear after a resim.
parser_notes: Clear AAR with a follow-on section folded into the same record.
